questions.q: What is the main advantage of using condoms as a contraceptive method?
questions.answer: The main advantage of using condoms is that they not only prevent unwanted pregnancy but also provide protection against sexually transmitted diseases (STDs).

questions.q: Which of the following contraceptive methods is not a natural method of family planning?
Describe the structure and function of a condom.
questions.answer: A condom is a sheath made of thin rubber or latex that is worn over the penis during sexual intercourse. It prevents the sperm from entering the female reproductive tract, thereby preventing fertilization and subsequent pregnancy. Condoms are also effective in preventing the transmission of sexually transmitted diseases (STDs).

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The ______________ method of family planning involves avoiding sexual intercourse from day 10 to 17 of a 28-day menstrual cycle.
What are the benefits of using IUDs as a contraceptive method?
questions.answer: The benefits of using IUDs include high effectiveness, ease of use, and long-term protection against pregnancy. IUDs are also reversible, making them a popular choice among women.

questions.q: True or False: Oral contraceptive pills can be used as an emergency contraceptive.
Explain the mechanism of action of oral contraceptive pills.
questions.answer: Oral contraceptive pills work by inhibiting the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) from the hypothalamus, which in turn reduces the production of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) from the pituitary gland. This prevents ovulation and thereby prevents fertilization and subsequent pregnancy.

questions.q: Which of the following is a medical termination of pregnancy method?
What are the advantages and disadvantages of using sterilization as a contraceptive method?
questions.answer: The advantages of sterilization include high effectiveness, permanence, and lack of hormonal side effects. However, the disadvantages include irreversibility, surgical risks, and potential regret.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The ______________ method of family planning involves calculating the fertile period of a woman based on her basal body temperature.
